join_order.build: nation(25 rows → direct array: nationkey → nation_name_idx)
part(filtered by p_name LIKE '%green%' → ~20K rows → bitset on p_partkey, domain 1-2M)
prebuilt_index_load(idx_orders_orderkey) — orders hash index mmap'd, 0ms build
join_order.note: Single hash probe per lineitem row; extract year via O(1) YEAR_TABLE lookup

join_order.build: prebuilt_index_load(idx_partsupp_part_supp) — partsupp multi-value hash index mmap'd, 0ms build
join_order.note: Combined (partkey, suppkey) key lookup; each (part, supp) pair is unique in partsupp so single-value result

logical_plan.aggregation: flat_array_direct (25 nations × ~7 years = ~175 groups, indexed by nation_idx * MAX_YEAR_SLOTS + year_slot)

late_materialization.rationale: Load p_name strings during part scan for LIKE filter; only emit p_partkey (int32) into bitset. Avoids carrying strings into join pipeline.

date_extraction.rationale: Avoids 30-iteration loop per row × 60M rows = 1.8B wasted operations

data_structures.nation_lookup: flat_array[25]: int32_t nationkey → const char* nation_name (direct array, fits in L1)
data_structures.supplier_nation_map: flat_array[100001]: int32_t suppkey → uint8_t nation_idx (direct array, suppkey domain 1-100K, 100KB total, fits in L2)
data_structures.part_filter_bitset: bitset(2097152 bits = 256KB): bit[p_partkey] = 1 if p_name LIKE '%green%', fits in L2 cache
data_structures.orders_index: prebuilt_index_load(idx_orders_orderkey) — mmap hash index, capacity 33,554,432, o_orderkey → o_orderdate (int32_t)
data_structures.partsupp_index: prebuilt_index_load(idx_partsupp_part_supp) — mmap multi-value hash index, capacity 4,194,304, (ps_partkey, ps_suppkey) → ps_supplycost (int64_t)
data_structures.aggregation_buffer: flat_array[250]: int64_t sum_profit[nation_idx * 10 + year_slot] — 250 × 8B = 2KB, fits in L1

phases.parallelism: parallel scan (2M rows), thread-local bitsets OR-merged after (bitset OR is trivial)

phases.per_thread: thread-local flat agg array[250], no contention on shared state
phases.synchronization: thread_local_merge — final sequential merge of 64 small arrays (64 × 2KB = 128KB, trivial)

index_usage.purpose: O(1) lookup of o_orderdate for each l_orderkey during lineitem scan; eliminates runtime hash table build on 15M orders rows
O(1) lookup of ps_supplycost for (l_partkey, l_suppkey) pair during lineitem scan; eliminates runtime hash table build on 8M partsupp rows

filter_order: 1. probe part_bitset[l_partkey] — branch-free bit test, skip ~99% of rows
5. compute amount = l_extendedprice * (100 - l_discount) / 100 - ps_supplycost * l_quantity (integer arithmetic, scale=100^2)
lineitem.expected_passing_rows: ~600K (1% of 60M pass the part bitset filter)

part.strategy: full_scan with substring match on p_name, emit p_partkey into bitset

supplier.strategy: full_scan, direct array lookup on nation, fill supplier_nation_map flat array

physical_plan.memory_pattern: streaming_sequential for lineitem scan; random_access_with_prefetch for hash index probes

arithmetic.integer_scale: all values in int64_t with scale_factor=100; amount is in units of 1/100 of currency
arithmetic.note: No floating point needed; divide by 10000 at output for decimal display

estimated_cost.dominant_phase: parallel_lineitem_scan (60M rows × hash probe + bitset test)

phase_breakdown.lineitem_parallel_scan: ~100-130ms (60M rows / 64 threads, with ~99% early-exit on part bitset)

estimated_cost.target_comparison: Umbra 196ms — aiming to beat via prebuilt index loads (0ms vs 500ms+ build) + part bitset eliminating 99% of lineitem rows early + flat array aggregation (0 hash collisions)
